title: helleborus lividus subsp. corsicus
intro: Helleborus lividus subsp. corsicus, also known as Corsican hellebore, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is a perennial plant with nodding, bell-shaped flowers that bloom in shades of green and white. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. The Corsican hellebore can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or cream, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
